sourceMovie.title: Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie works as a science fiction movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The science fiction and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Resident Evil: Degeneration
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie clicked for you, Resident Evil: Degeneration (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and animation, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Batman: Gotham Knight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie clicked for you, Batman: Gotham Knight (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in science fiction and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie?
faq.answer: Try Resident Evil: Degeneration, Batman: Gotham Knight, Appleseed—each shares DNA with Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie?
faq.answer: Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie (2015) is best described as Science Fiction, Animation. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Ghost in the Shell: The New Movie part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Ghost in the Shell: Arise Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
